title: Invalidate stale classification outcomes
description: Make prior outcomes unappliable whenever any canonical input represented by their Evaluation Fingerprint changes. Implements G2, G3, and UC-CLASSIFY-002/003.
rationale: A once-correct suggestion can become unsafe after lifecycle, category, contract, normalization, or rule changes.
story_so_that: an old preview cannot mutate current Ledger state
acceptance_criteria: Given a transaction becomes void in public LEDGER state, when a prior outcome is inspected or applied, then CLASSIFY reports it stale and blocks apply.
Given a transaction is superseded in public LEDGER state, when a prior outcome is inspected or applied, then CLASSIFY reports it stale and blocks apply.
Given a transaction allocation changes in public LEDGER state, when a prior outcome is inspected or applied, then CLASSIFY reports it stale and blocks apply.
Given a transaction's transfer relationship changes in public LEDGER state, when a prior outcome is inspected or applied, then CLASSIFY reports it stale and blocks apply.
Given a transaction's refund or reversal relationship changes in public LEDGER state, when a prior outcome is inspected or applied, then CLASSIFY reports it stale and blocks apply.
Given a targeted category is archived, when a prior outcome is inspected or applied, then CLASSIFY reports it stale and blocks apply.
Given a targeted category is reactivated, when a prior outcome created before reactivation is inspected or applied, then CLASSIFY reports it stale and blocks apply.
Given a targeted category identity changes, when a prior outcome is inspected or applied, then CLASSIFY reports it stale and blocks apply.
Given a category rename that preserves the same active identifier, when a prior outcome is inspected, then its category identity remains valid and the current display name is returned.

Given the required LEDGER contract or classification projection version changes, when a prior outcome is inspected or applied, then CLASSIFY identifies that changed fingerprint dimension and blocks apply.
Given the LEDGER store generation changes, when a prior outcome is inspected or applied, then CLASSIFY identifies that changed fingerprint dimension and blocks apply.
Given the source LEDGER snapshot is invalid or expired, when a prior outcome is inspected or applied, then CLASSIFY identifies that changed fingerprint dimension and blocks apply.
Given the normalization version changes, when a prior outcome is inspected or applied, then CLASSIFY identifies that changed fingerprint dimension and blocks apply.
Given the active Rule Set Version changes, when a prior outcome is inspected or applied, then CLASSIFY identifies that changed fingerprint dimension and blocks apply.
Given a stale outcome, when the owner requests a safe next action, then CLASSIFY identifies re-evaluation rather than correction or forced apply.
